commit.message:
Fix performance issues in news and filings backfill

- Use executemany() for batch inserts in backfill_news_polygon,
  backfill_news_finnhub, and backfill_8k_for_ticker, replacing per-record
  execute() loops. News is the highest-volume dataset so this has the
  most impact.

- Add limit=1000 param to fetch_8k_filings to reduce pagination round-trips,
  mirroring the same fix applied to corporate actions endpoints.

- Eliminate redundant dict copy in _extract_sentiment: was doing
  enriched = dict(article) on every article before adding 2 keys.
  Since news.py re-extracts sentiment from insights itself, the copy was
  wasted work. Now mutates in-place instead.

commit.message:
Fix audit logs real-time updates and OMR scanner alignment error handling

- Replace one-time getDocs fetch with onSnapshot in AuditLogs.tsx for real-time updates
- Add subscribeLogs() method to AuditLogger service using Firestore onSnapshot
- Show alignment error on camera mode instead of results mode
- Return early when corner markers not detected to prevent misaligned bubble markers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

📝 docs: clarify automatic tool installation flow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix memory leak in corporate actions backfill

- Cache tenacity retry wrapper as self._retrying_execute in PolygonClient.__init__
  instead of recreating a new closure on every HTTP request. Hundreds of wrapper
  objects were accumulating during pagination loops, causing GC pressure and slowness.

- Add limit=1000 param to fetch_dividends, fetch_splits, fetch_short_interest to
  reduce pagination round-trips (Polygon default page size is small).

- Use executemany() for batch inserts in all three backfill_*_for_ticker functions
  instead of a per-record execute() loop.

- Wrap PolygonClient in a with context manager in run_full_backfill so the
  httpx.Client connection pool is always released on exit.

- Add regression tests: retry wrapper identity check across requests, limit param
  assertions on all three corporate actions fetch methods.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Charts: Extract shared ChartLayout component for chart+legend layout (#47554)

* Charts: Add ChartLayout component for shared chart+legend layout

Introduces a ChartLayout component that encapsulates the shared
Stack + legend positioning pattern used by all chart components.
Includes comprehensive tests covering legend positioning, visibility
management, ref forwarding, and composition API slot rendering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Export ChartLayoutProps type from barrel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate LineChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate BarChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate PieChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate PieSemiCircleChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate LeaderboardChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Restore responsive/loading classes in LeaderboardChart empty state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: PieChart uses ChartLayout render prop for measurement

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Add render prop and measurement support to ChartLayout

ChartLayout now accepts children as either ReactNode or a render prop
receiving ContentMeasurements (contentRef, contentWidth, contentHeight,
isMeasured). This allows charts to consolidate their useElementSize
calls into the shared layout component.

Adds waitForMeasurement prop that hides the layout until content is
measured. The legacy isWaitingForMeasurement prop is preserved as
deprecated for backward compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Fix PieChart SingleChartContext.Provider placement

Move SingleChartContext.Provider to wrap ChartLayout (outside) so
composition API legends in legend slots can access the chart context.
Keep inner provider inside the render prop for measured dimensions.

Remove waitForMeasurement since PieChart uses fallback dimensions
(300x300) when unmeasured and doesn't need to hide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate LineChart, BarChart, PieSemiCircleChart to ChartLayout render prop

Remove individual useElementSize calls from each chart and use
ChartLayout's render prop to receive content measurements instead.

Pattern: outer SingleChartContext.Provider with fallback dimensions
(for composition API legends in layout slots), inner provider inside
render prop with measured dimensions (for chart internals).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add changelog entries.

* test(charts): Simplify ChartLayout DOM order assertions

Replace the expectDomOrder helper with direct compareDocumentPosition
assertions. The helper required eslint-disable no-bitwise for the
masking check; inline expect(...).toBe(Node.DOCUMENT_POSITION_FOLLOWING)
is clearer and avoids the suppression.

Made-with: Cursor

* Charts: Extract shared CSS into ChartLayout, use Stack for pie centering

ChartLayout now wraps render prop children in a content div with shared
flex styles (flex: 1; min-height: 0; min-width: 0), removing duplicated
svg-wrapper rules from line-chart, bar-chart, pie-chart, and
pie-semi-circle-chart SCSS modules.

Charts no longer need to attach contentRef — ChartLayout handles
measurement internally via the content wrapper div.

Replace custom flex centering CSS in pie-chart and pie-semi-circle-chart
svg-wrappers with Stack components using align/justify props, per
project guidelines preferring layout primitives over ad-hoc flex rules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Remove redundant flex and inline styles from pie charts

The pie-chart class had `display: flex; flex-direction: column;
align-items: center` which duplicated what the Stack component
already provides. The `align-items: center` also prevented the
chart-layout content area from stretching to full width.

Similarly, the centering Stack inside both pie charts had inline
`style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}` that is unnecessary
now that ChartLayout's content wrapper handles flex sizing.

Also removes hardcoded `size` props from two pie chart stories
that were masking the responsive sizing behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(charts): Remove superpowers plan from tracked files

Superpowers directories are now ignored via .git/info/exclude.
Removing the previously tracked plan file so it stays local.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Move ChartLayout to charts/private

ChartLayout is internal infrastructure consumed only by chart
components, not a public API. Moving it alongside other internal
modules (chart-composition, single-chart-context, with-responsive)
makes its scope clear and avoids exposing it in the components
directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Pass measured chart height to SingleChartContext in LineChart

SingleChartContext.chartHeight was set to the full container height
(including legend space), but AnnotationsOverlay uses this value to
size its SVG overlay. This caused misaligned annotations when a legend
was present because the overlay was sized larger than the actual chart
drawing area.

Lift the measured content height from the ChartLayout render prop into
state so the context provider reflects the true drawable area.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Add full dimensions to pie chart centering Stack

The centering Stack inside the ChartLayout render prop had no explicit
dimensions, so justify="center" had no effect because the Stack
collapsed to content height. Adding width/height 100% makes the Stack
fill the measured content area, enabling proper vertical centering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(charts): Fix misleading isMeasured docstring in ChartLayout

The docstring claimed isMeasured is "always true when
waitForMeasurement is not set", but isMeasured derives from
contentHeight > 0, which is false on initial render before
ResizeObserver fires regardless of waitForMeasurement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(charts): Add ChartLayout render-prop and waitForMeasurement tests

Cover the render-prop measurement behavior (function-as-children
receives contentWidth/contentHeight/isMeasured) and verify that
waitForMeasurement takes precedence over the legacy
isWaitingForMeasurement prop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Remove legacy isWaitingForMeasurement prop from ChartLayout

No callers pass isWaitingForMeasurement as a prop to ChartLayout —
line-chart and bar-chart only use it as a local variable name. Simplify
the visibility logic to only support waitForMeasurement and update
tests accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Use useEffect callback instead of setState during render

Calling setMeasuredChartHeight inside ChartLayout's render prop
triggered a React warning: "Cannot update a component while rendering
a different component". Add an onContentHeightChange callback prop to
ChartLayout that fires via useEffect after measurement, avoiding the
cross-component state update during render.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(charts): Document waitForMeasurement render-prop requirement

waitForMeasurement only works with render-prop children because
contentRef is only attached in that code path. With plain ReactNode
children, isMeasured stays false and the layout remains permanently
hidden. Add JSDoc warning to prevent silent misuse.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Remove unused chartWidth/chartHeight from pie and leaderboard context

Pie, semi-circle, and leaderboard charts don't have consumers that
read chartWidth/chartHeight from SingleChartContext. Passing zeros was
misleading. Make the fields optional on ChartInstanceContextValue and
omit them from charts that don't provide meaningful values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Pass measured chart height to SingleChartContext in BarChart

Same pattern as LineChart: track the measured content height via
onContentHeightChange callback so SingleChartContext.chartHeight
reflects the actual drawable area, not the full container height.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Auto-hide ChartLayout during measurement for render-prop children

Replace the opt-in waitForMeasurement prop with automatic behavior:
when children is a render prop, ChartLayout applies visibility:hidden
until contentHeight > 0. This prevents layout shift consistently
across all charts without each chart needing to opt in.

Plain ReactNode children are unaffected since they don't depend on
measured dimensions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Simplify measurement gating, mock element size in tests

ChartLayout now auto-hides render-prop children until measured, so
individual charts no longer need their own isWaitingForMeasurement
logic. Simplify bar/line charts to gate on chartHeight > 0 directly
and remove the now-unused hasLegend variables.

Add a getBoundingClientRect + ResizeObserver mock to the test setup so
useElementSize returns non-zero dimensions in JSDOM. Without this, the
auto-hide visibility guard keeps charts permanently hidden in tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Remove redundant visx tooltip mock

The setup-visx-tooltip-mock.js mock overrode useTooltipInPortal to
provide non-zero containerBounds because JSDOM's getBoundingClientRect
returns zeros. Now that setup-element-size-mock.js globally mocks
getBoundingClientRect to return 800x400, the visx mock is redundant —
react-use-measure (used internally by useTooltipInPortal) gets non-zero
values directly from the source.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style(charts): Move pie chart centering styles to CSS modules

Inline styles bypass the CSS module system and make overrides harder.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Fix 6 test failures from element size mock

The setup-element-size-mock.js introduced in e242b7d had three flaws:

1. Accessed Element.prototype unconditionally, crashing the SSR test
   (jest-environment node) with "Element is not defined"

2. Returned hardcoded 800×400 for ALL zero-sized elements, overriding
   explicit dimensions (e.g. Sparkline at 100×40 got height 400)

3. The visx tooltip mock removed in 92201ce was NOT redundant —
   react-use-measure has a mounted.current guard that prevents state
   updates when ResizeObserver fires synchronously during the ref
   callback, leaving containerBounds at {0,0,...} and suppressing
   all tooltips

Fixes:
- Guard Element access with typeof check for SSR compatibility
- Walk up DOM tree for ancestor inline style dimensions instead of
  hardcoded fallback, so charts with explicit sizes measure correctly
- Restore visx tooltip mock with updated documentation explaining
  the react-use-measure timing issue
- Override getBoundingClientRect locally in chart-layout visibility
  test to simulate unmeasured state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Guard onContentHeightChange for render-prop mode

When children is a plain ReactNode, the content measurement ref is
never attached, so contentHeight stays 0. Firing
onContentHeightChange(0) in that case is misleading for callers who
wire up the callback without realizing it only produces meaningful
values in render-prop mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(charts): Wrap mock override in try/finally

Ensures the global getBoundingClientRect mock is restored even if
an assertion throws, preventing leak into subsequent tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Only call onContentHeightChange if measured

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(charts): Enhance early return condition in LineChartAnnotationsOverlay

Updated the early return condition to include checks for chartWidth and chartHeight, ensuring that the component only renders when all necessary dimensions are available. This prevents potential rendering issues when the chart is not properly sized.

* fix(charts): Pass args through in composition legend stories

Composition legend stories for bar, pie, donut, and semi-circle
were cherry-picking individual props instead of spreading args.
This meant Storybook controls like showLegend had no effect,
preventing dual legend rendering (showLegend + composition
<Chart.Legend>) that works in line chart and leaderboard stories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

correct dual-curve chart showing Innovation Potential (cyan dashed) vs Adoption
Risk (amber solid) with the Leading Edge → Mainstream target zone.

Also enriches the lifecycle stage definitions in the markdown content with

Chasm), Gartner Hype Cycle, NIST SP 800-160, Microsoft Lifecycle Policy,

Add three substantive sections to slide 6 (Technology Lifecycle Positioning):

1. "Reading the Chart" — explains the dual-curve relationship between
   innovation potential and adoption risk, citing Christensen (Innovator's
   Dilemma), Gartner failure rates at Innovation Trigger, NIST Cybersecurity
   Framework 2.0 on aging infrastructure risk, and Rogers' 68% adoption
   window for early/late majority.

2. "Real-World Examples" — a 12-row table mapping concrete technologies
   (WebTransport, PQC, Rust, Deno/Bun, Node.js, React, PostgreSQL, jQuery,
   AngularJS, Windows 10, CentOS 7, Python 2.7) to their lifecycle stage
   with evidence from Stack Overflow 2024, DB-Engines, Gartner, NIST,

* feat: add lifecycle timeline charts for hardware, software, and supply chain examples

Add three new optional deep-dive slides (27-29) with proportional
timeline bar charts showing real technologies progressing through each
lifecycle phase. Each chart includes sourced phase durations that
explain why real adoption curves are asymmetric (imperfect S-curves):

- Slide 27: Hardware (HDDs) — 77-year lifecycle, right-skewed curve
  with 30-year mainstream plateau and rapid SSD displacement
- Slide 28: Software (Adobe Flash) — 25-year lifecycle, left-skewed

The previous ?? fallback meant underscore emphasis was only detected when

matched independently and the existing sort-by-index picks whichever
appears first, correctly handling mixed cases like `_a_ then *b*`.

Replace with (^|[^\w])_..._([^\w]|$) and adjust the match object so
downstream tokenization code sees the same group layout.

Create the 4 shared packages that apps already import from:

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Pytorch] Add ignore_raw_node option to GraphPickler (#176939)

Summary:
The Graph Pickler raises "Unexpected raw Node during pickling" when it encounters a raw `torch.fx.Node` during serialization. This occurs in some backward graphs (e.g. IDXCVR model__27_backward_7) where metadata contains Node references that haven't been converted to `_NodePickleData`.

This diff adds an `ignore_raw_node` option to `GraphPickler.Options` that, when enabled, silently pickles unexpected raw Nodes as `None` instead of raising an `AssertionError`. This allows graph serialization to finish even when graphs contain these edge-case references.

Test Plan: buck test fbcode//mtia/accuracy/training/tests:test_graph_serialize; arc lint — no new issues

Reviewed By: aorenste

Differential Revision: D95510476

Pull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/176939
Approved by: https://github.com/aorenste

commit.message:
Implement ERGM parsing engine: parse_ergm_model() and parse_ergm_formula() (#6)

* Initial plan

* Implement parse_ergm_model() and parse_ergm_formula() with tests

Core ERGM parsing engine with:
- Formula parsing (term extraction, attribute detection)
- Model extraction (coefficients, SEs, p-values)
- Coefficient-to-term mapping for expanded terms
- Term metadata lookup via ergm::search.ergmTerms()
- Standardized output data frame

Co-authored-by: gvegayon <893619+gvegayon@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review: switch to tinytest, add Makefile, handle multi-attribute terms, remove requireNamespace check, add bipartite/mixing/edges tests

Co-authored-by: gvegayon <893619+gvegayon@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add clarifying comment on mixing term prefix test

Co-authored-by: gvegayon <893619+gvegayon@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CI: use ergm_model() for accurate coefficient-to-term mapping

The prefix matching in .match_coef_to_term() fails for terms where ergm's
coefficient names differ from the formula term name:
- nodemix produces coefficients like mix.role.A.B (prefix "mix", not "nodemix")
- b1star(2) produces coefficient b1star2 (no dot separator)

New .map_coefs_to_terms() uses ergm::ergm_model() which provides the
authoritative term-to-coefficient mapping via $terms[[i]]$coef.names.
Falls back to prefix matching if ergm_model() is unavailable.

Co-authored-by: gvegayon <893619+gvegayon@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve .map_coefs_to_terms: validate name alignment, use list for lookup

Co-authored-by: gvegayon <893619+gvegayon@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix b1star mapping: remove strict name validation, add non-dot prefix matching

Three changes to fix the b1star coefficient mapping failure:

1. Removed the overly strict name validation check in .map_coefs_to_terms()
   that was skipping terms where ergm_model's internal name didn't exactly
   match the formula-parsed name.

2. Changed .map_coefs_to_terms() to fall back to .match_coef_to_term() for
   individual unmatched coefficients (instead of returning NA), so partial
   ergm_model coverage still allows prefix matching for the rest.

3. Added non-dot prefix matching as a final fallback in .match_coef_to_term()
   to handle terms like b1star(2) which produce coefficient names like
   "b1star2" (no dot separator). The dot-prefix match is tried first to
   avoid false positives.

Co-authored-by: gvegayon <893619+gvegayon@users.noreply.github.com>
